questions.q: What is the primary function of the Central Processing Unit (CPU)?
questions.answer: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is the primary component of a computer system that executes most instructions that a computer receives. It fetches instructions, decodes them, and carries out the required operations. The CPU consists of Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) and Control Unit (CU).

questions.q: What is the difference between a Bus and a Channel in a computer system?
questions.answer: A Bus is a common path through which data is transmitted between components of a computer system, whereas a Channel is a dedicated path for data transfer between two specific devices. Buses are shared resources, whereas Channels are not.

questions.q: What is the role of the Input/Output (I/O) devices in a computer system?
questions.answer: Input/Output (I/O) devices are peripherals that allow users to interact with the computer system. They include keyboards, mice, monitors, and printers, which enable users to input data and receive output from the system.

questions.q: Explain the concept of Virtual Memory in a computer system.
questions.answer: Virtual Memory is an extension of the computer's Random Access Memory (RAM) that uses a portion of the Hard Disk for temporary storage. It allows the operating system to use more memory than is actually available, improving system performance and efficiency.

questions.q: What is the difference between a Monoprocessor and a Multiprocessor system?
questions.answer: A Monoprocessor system has only one Processor, whereas a Multiprocessor system has multiple Processors that work together to improve system performance and speed up task execution.

questions.q: Explain the concept of Cache Memory in a computer system.
questions.answer: Cache Memory is a small, fast memory that stores frequently used data and instructions. It acts as a buffer between the Main Memory and the CPU, significantly improving system performance by reducing the time taken to access data.

questions.q: What is the role of the Operating System in a computer system?
questions.answer: The Operating System (OS) is a software that manages computer hardware resources and provides services to computer programs. It acts as an interface between the user and the computer hardware, enabling efficient and secure operation of the system.

questions.q: Explain the concept of Scheduling in a computer system.
questions.answer: Scheduling is the process of allocating system resources, such as CPU time, to various tasks or programs. It ensures that tasks are executed efficiently and that the system operates smoothly, without conflicts or deadlocks.

questions.q: What is the difference between a Process and a Thread in a computer system?
questions.answer: A Process is an independent program in execution, whereas a Thread is a subset of a Process that can execute concurrently with other threads within the same Process. Threads share the same memory space, whereas Processes have separate memory spaces.

questions.q: Explain the concept of Interrupts in a computer system.
questions.answer: Interrupts are signals sent to the CPU to interrupt its current activity and attend to a higher-priority task or event. They enable the system to respond quickly to external events, improving overall system performance and efficiency.
